title: Revitalizing Roasted Japanese Lemon Kushikatsu
description: A citrusy and springy Japanese dish featuring lemon, perfect for a hangover cure, made with Buddhist temple friendly ingredients in a kushikatsu form

ingredients.name: Japanese lemons
ingredients.notes: or regular lemons if Japanese lemons are not available

instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together flour, sake, mirin, sugar, and salt.
Add the water to the bowl and mix until a smooth batter forms.
Cut the Japanese lemons into 1 inch pieces and dip each piece into the batter, then roll in panko breadcrumbs to coat.
Heat the vegetable oil in a deep frying pan to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
Fry the lemon pieces in batches until golden brown, about 2-3 minutes per batch.
Remove the fried lemon pieces from the oil and place on a paper towel lined plate to drain excess oil.
In a small bowl, whisk together sesame oil and soy sauce for the dipping sauce.
Serve the kushikatsu hot with the dipping sauce and garnished with chopped scallions.
Roast the kushikatsu in the oven for an additional 5 minutes to give it a char-grill flavor.
